chapter_context.summary: Declares that heaven is God's throne and the humble spirit is His delight, contrasts formal sacrifices with true worship, and depicts the final destinies of all.

outline.text: Heaven as God's Throne; Humility vs. Formal Hypocrisy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Heaven as God's Throne; Humility vs. Formal Hypocrisy (66:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Sudden Birth of Zion's Children; Comfort like Mother
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Sudden Birth of Zion's Children; Comfort like Mother (66:5-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Lord Coming with Fire and Sword; Universal Worship
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Lord Coming with Fire and Sword; Universal Worship (66:15-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Glory and Future Hope
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of glory and future hope, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 66, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
